title: quercus cinerea var. humilis
intro: Quercus cinerea var. humilis, also known as the Desert Live Oak, is a shrub or small tree that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Quercus cinerea var. humilis include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote healthy digestion, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Quercus cinerea var. humilis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
